subject: Product Management
title: Figma
description: A short lesson to help you apply Figma.

hook.text: Adobe owned design tools for 30 years. Figma disrupted them by moving design from desktop software to the browser.

buildup.text: Dylan Field dropped out of college to build a design tool that ran entirely in a web browser — no installation needed.

discovery.text: Figma's genius wasn't the browser — it was real-time collaboration. Multiple designers editing one file simultaneously.

twist.text: Adobe's Photoshop and Illustrator were more powerful. But Figma won because collaboration beat individual power tools.

climax.text: Adobe tried to acquire Figma for $20 billion — the largest design tool acquisition ever — acknowledging Figma had won.

punchline.text: Collaboration in the browser beat power on the desktop.

quiz.question: What product insight let Figma disrupt Adobe?
options: Real-time collaboration in the browser beat individual desktop power tools
Figma had more features than Photoshop
Figma was the first design tool ever created
